title: oboe-mcp/CLI Agent Usability Improvements
description: Evaluation of MCP server and CLI for agent-facing improvements: reduced token usage, eliminated round trips, better error surfaces, first-class tool gaps, and code quality fixes.

items.title: Fix obo_complete_child_session wrong parent key
items.description: obo_complete_child_session returns parent_session.get("session_file") for parent_session_file in the JSON response, but the session dict does not reliably have a "session_file" key — that field is the filename used to load the session, not stored inside it. Should use child_session.get("parent_session_file") instead. This means the parent_session_file in the response is currently None or wrong.
items.resolution: Fixed: use child_session.get('parent_session_file') instead of parent_session.get('session_file')

items.title: obo_next: include progress context in response
items.description: obo_next returns only the item dict with no indication of how many items remain. Agents always have to issue a second obo_session_status call to know progress. Adding {"progress": {"completed": N, "total": M, "remaining": K}} to the obo_next response eliminates this round trip.
items.resolution: Added progress {completed, total, remaining} to obo_next item response; also added action_required hint to all-blocked case

items.title: CLI: --json global output flag
items.description: Every CLI command outputs decorated human text (==== banners, icon characters, tabular layout). Agents must text-parse this, burning tokens and risking misreads. A --json flag returns a stable machine-readable dict from every subcommand. This is the single biggest agent-facing CLI improvement. All other CLI improvements become significantly more useful once this exists.
items.approval_note: Approved for implementation; medium effort, highest CLI token-reduction value

items.title: obo_next: add mark_in_progress param
items.description: Getting the next item and marking it in-progress are always paired operations. An optional mark_in_progress=True parameter on obo_next would atomically return the next item AND mark it in_progress, eliminating one round-trip tool call from every iteration of the work loop.
items.resolution: Added mark_in_progress=False param to obo_next; aliased import to mark_in_progress_fn to avoid name collision

items.title: CLI: --items inline JSON for create/merge/create-child
items.description: create, merge, and create-child all require --input-file FILE, forcing agents to write a temp file first. Add --items '[{...}]' to accept an inline JSON string, and support --items - to read from stdin. Eliminates the write-temp-file boilerplate entirely.
items.resolution: Added --items JSON/- to create, merge, create-child as an alternative to --input-file (mutually exclusive group)

items.title: obo_mark_complete: return structured progress not string
items.description: obo_mark_complete returns "progress": "3/12" as a string. Agents have to string-parse it to extract numbers. Should return {"completed": 3, "total": 12, "remaining": 9} as a structured dict instead.
items.resolution: obo_mark_complete progress field changed from "N/T" string to {completed, total, remaining} dict; test updated

items.title: CLI: auto-generate child session filename
items.description: create-child requires --child-session FILENAME, forcing agents to construct a conforming session_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.json timestamp themselves. The CLI already auto-generates filenames for create; create-child should do the same when --child-session is omitted, matching the MCP server behavior.
items.resolution: Made --child-session optional; CLI auto-generates session_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.json when omitted, matching MCP behaviour

items.title: obo_complete_session: return final item counts
items.description: obo_complete_session returns only {status, action, session_file, session_status}. Agents typically call obo_session_status immediately after to report totals. Including {completed, skipped, total} in the completion response eliminates that follow-up call.
items.resolution: obo_complete_session now returns completed/skipped/total counts in response; test updated to assert all three fields

items.title: CLI: auto-infer session when unambiguous
items.description: When --session is omitted and exactly one active session exists in the sessions dir, silently infer it. Every item-level command currently requires the full --session session_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.json argument even when there is only one session. This is the largest token overhead in typical agent usage patterns.
items.resolution: When --session omitted and exactly one non-completed/cancelled session exists, _require_session auto-infers it; errors with list if multiple active

items.title: CLI: next --mark-in-progress flag
items.description: Same as the MCP change: getting the next item and marking it in-progress are always paired. A --mark-in-progress flag on the next subcommand reduces two CLI calls to one.
items.resolution: Added --mark-in-progress flag to CLI next subcommand; marks item in-progress atomically; added test_next_mark_in_progress_flag test

items.title: obo_next: improve all-blocked response
items.description: When all remaining items are blocked, obo_next returns {"message": "No actionable items remain; all unresolved items are blocked", "blocked": N}. Agents are left guessing what to do next. Should add "action_required": "Call obo_list_items with status_filter='blocked' to review blockers" to give explicit next-step guidance, matching the pattern used for the paused-by-child case.
items.resolution: Implemented as part of item 2 (mcp-next-progress) — all-blocked response now includes action_required hint

items.title: CLI: error output as JSON in --json mode
items.description: When --json is active, errors should output {"status": "error", "message": "...", "exit_code": 1} to stdout (not stderr) and exit non-zero. Currently errors always print as plain text to stderr regardless of output mode, forcing agents to check two streams. Depends on --json flag being implemented first.
items.skip_reason: Blocked: depends on cli-json-flag which is approved-delayed (deferred)

items.title: MCP: add obo_mark_deferred first-class tool
items.description: deferred is a valid lifecycle status but has no first-class tool — agents must use obo_update_field(field="status", value="deferred") which is verbose and easy to misuse. Adding obo_mark_deferred(session_file, item_id, reason?) makes deferral as ergonomic as obo_mark_skip or obo_mark_blocked. Should also accept an optional deferred_until date hint.
items.resolution: Added mark_deferred() to session.py and obo_mark_deferred MCP tool with reason + deferred_until params; tests added

items.title: obo_list_sessions: include active_child_session in rows
items.description: When an agent calls obo_list_sessions to find which session to resume, it cannot immediately tell if a session is paused by an active child session. Including active_child_session in each index row lets the agent navigate directly to the child without a second call.
items.resolution: active_child_session already present in _upsert_index rows; added assertion to test_obo_list_sessions to document and enforce it

items.title: MCP: add obo_get_session for title/description/metadata
items.description: There is no tool to retrieve a session's title, description, and creation metadata. obo_session_status returns only item counts; obo_list_sessions returns a summary row; neither gives the full session header. A new obo_get_session(session_file, base_dir?) tool returning {title, description, created, status, parent_session_file, child_session_files, active_child_session} fills this gap.
items.resolution: Added obo_get_session tool returning title/description/created/status/parent+child relationship fields; load_session added to server.py imports; test added

items.title: obo_list_sessions: document/unify 'incomplete' filter
items.description: obo_list_sessions accepts status_filter="incomplete" (meaning active+paused with open items) but obo_trim_sessions uses "any" to mean all statuses. This split vocabulary requires agents to know which terms apply to which tool. Should ensure SKILL.md clearly documents that "incomplete" is only for list_sessions, and consider whether obo_trim_sessions should also accept "incomplete".
items.resolution: Documented vocabulary split in docstrings: 'incomplete' for list_sessions, 'any' for trim_sessions; SKILL.md updated with all new tools and corrected param names

items.title: CLI: complete --resolution flag instead of positional
items.description: complete takes 'item_id resolution [resolution...]' as positionals. Multi-word resolutions must be passed as separate words which argparse joins, but the pattern is fragile and hard for agents to quote correctly. A --resolution TEXT flag is unambiguous: oboe-cli complete 3 --resolution "multi-word resolution".
items.resolution: Replaced positional resolution with required --resolution TEXT flag; no backward compat; tests updated

items.title: CLI: status --compact one-liner output
items.description: The status command outputs an 8-line table with ==== borders. For inline status checks during loops, a single-line compact form "3/12 done (25%) — 2 in-progress, 4 pending, 1 blocked" saves significant output tokens. Add --compact flag.
items.resolution: Added --compact flag to status subcommand; outputs single line: N/T done (P%) — X in-progress, Y pending, Z blocked

items.title: MCP: fix inline import in obo_cancel_session
items.description: obo_cancel_session contains 'from oboe_mcp.session import _open_count' inside the function body. This is a code smell — private symbols imported locally on every call, hidden from the module's import block, and a minor performance cost on repeated calls. Move to the top-level import block.
items.resolution: Moved _open_count from inline import inside obo_cancel_session to top-level import block

items.title: CLI: sessions --active shorthand flag
items.description: sessions --status active is the most common query pattern and the most verbose. A bare --active flag saves tokens in agent prompts/instructions. Minor convenience improvement.
items.resolution: Added --active shorthand flag to sessions subcommand; test added

items.title: CLI: show --fields to limit output columns
items.description: show ITEM_ID dumps every field of an item, including many null/empty fields. A --fields id,title,status filter limits output to just the named fields, reducing tokens when agents only need a subset.
items.resolution: Added --fields flag to show subcommand; filters item output to named fields; test added

items.title: MCP: update stale tool count in server docstring
items.description: The server module docstring says '16 tools for One-By-One session management' but the server now has 18 tools. Minor cosmetic fix.
items.resolution: Updated server docstring: 16 tools → 20 tools (actual count)

items.title: Rename note param to approval_note across MCP and CLI
items.description: obo_set_approval takes note= but stores to item["approval_note"]. Agents write via one name and read back under a different one. Fix: rename MCP param note->approval_note, CLI --note->--approval-note.
items.resolution: Renamed note→approval_note in server.py obo_set_approval, test_server.py, and test_cli.py (--note→--approval-note)

items.title: Rename MCP cancel reason param to cancel_reason
items.description: obo_cancel_session takes reason= but stores to session["cancel_reason"]. Fix: rename MCP param reason->cancel_reason to match JSON field. CLI positional stays as 'reason' (contextually unambiguous in cancel-session subcommand).
items.resolution: Renamed reason→cancel_reason in server.py obo_cancel_session and test_server.py

items.title: Rename session_filename param to session_file in obo_create and obo_create_child_session
items.description: obo_create and obo_create_child_session use session_filename= for the optional explicit filename, but responses and all other tools use session_file. Fix: rename param to session_file throughout MCP.
items.resolution: Renamed session_filename→session_file in server.py obo_create/obo_create_child_session and test_server.py (bulk sed)

items.title: Rename CLI approve --mode to --approval-mode
items.description: CLI approve uses --mode but MCP uses approval_mode and JSON field is approval_mode. --mode alone is ambiguous. Fix: rename CLI flag to --approval-mode (keeping dest=approval_mode).
items.resolution: Renamed --mode→--approval-mode in cli.py approve subcommand and test_cli.py
